title: Political orientation, environmental values, and climate change beliefs and attitudes: An empirical cross country analysis

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: support of publicly financed climate policy
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is general public financing of climate policy, not a carbon or fuel tax instrument.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: willingness to pay a price premium for climate-friendly products
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This measures consumer willingness to pay more for products, not tax support or tax preferences.

inclusion_reason: The paper studies climate beliefs and general climate-policy support, but no direct carbon tax, fuel tax, or equivalent tax instrument.
exclusion_reason: No carbon tax, CO2 tax, fuel tax, or TICPE-like excise is analyzed; only general climate policy support and price-premium willingness.
